title: Seymour's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
statement_text: For every apex-forest $H_1$ and outerplanar graph $H_2$ there is an integer $p$ such that every 2-connected graph of pathwidth at least $p$ contains $H_1$ or $H_2$ as a minor.
context_text: In 1993, Paul Seymour proposed this conjecture as a 2-connected analogue of the Robertson–Seymour theorem that every graph of large pathwidth contains any fixed forest as a minor. Equivalently, it characterises the minor-closed classes in which every 2-connected member has bounded pathwidth: precisely those excluding some apex-forest and some outerplanar graph. The motivation was also to seek evidence for a matroid version of the forest-pathwidth theorem.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full by establishing two explicit unavoidable families of minors ($\Gamma^+_\ell$ and $\nabla_\ell$) for 2-connected graphs of large pathwidth, and showing every apex-forest (resp. outerplanar graph) is a minor of a sufficiently large member of the first (resp. second) family.
notes: PDF source; statement text is prose-heavy with minimal display math and is cleanly legible. An independent proof by Dang and Thomas (arXiv:1712.04549) is noted in the abstract.
paper_title: Seymour's conjecture on 2-connected graphs of large pathwidth
